theme_archetype: Archetype 4: Swiss & High-Contrast (Light Theme) - Academic App Context
design_philosophy: Dense, data-first control room style. We are combining three vertically stacked, space-wasting cards into highly scanable, unified bento-box and horizontal strip layouts. Priority on optical alignment, compact vertical height, and clear hierarchy.

hierarchy.label: text-xs font-medium text-slate-500 uppercase tracking-wider
hierarchy.badge: text-[11px] font-medium px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full border

layout_options.name: Option 1: The Bento Row (Equal Split)
layout_options.description: A single row divided into 3 equal columns on desktop. Highly balanced, reads left to right.
layout_options.structure: flex flex-col md:flex-row divide-y md:divide-y-0 md:divide-x border rounded-xl bg-white shadow-sm overflow-hidden
sections: Left (Rating): Huge '9.0' with '/10' subscript. Below it, a dense 2x2 grid of the 4 sub-score badges.
Middle (Stats): Hero '92%' win rate. Below it, a flex row with '45W / 4L' in green/red, and '49 Matches' in slate.
Right (CI): Score '1677 ±77' text. Below it, the horizontal CI bar visualization.

layout_options.name: Option 2: The Hero Left Sidebar (Asymmetric)
layout_options.description: Prioritizes the Overall Rating and CI into a highlighted left column, grouping granular stats on the right.
layout_options.structure: flex flex-col md:flex-row border rounded-xl bg-white shadow-sm overflow-hidden
sections: Left Sidebar (w-1/3, bg-slate-50): Stacks Overall Rating (large) and the CI visualization directly underneath it.
Right Content (w-2/3, p-4, bg-white): A 2x2 grid layout. Top row: Sub-score badges wrapping naturally. Bottom row: Tournament stats displayed as mini-metrics (Wins, Losses, Rate).

layout_options.name: Option 3: The Compact Strip (Ultra-dense)
layout_options.description: Absolute minimum vertical height. Best if the paper detail page is heavily crowded. Designed to look like a stock ticker or complex table row.
layout_options.structure: flex flex-wrap md:flex-nowrap items-center justify-between p-3 border rounded-lg bg-white shadow-sm gap-4
sections: Item 1: Flex row with Overall Rating (text-2xl) and a vertical divider.
Item 2: Flex row of Sub-score badges (very tight gap-1).
Item 3: Vertical divider, then '92% Win Rate' and small '45W - 4L'.
Item 4: Vertical divider, then CI visual collapsed into a very small horizontal footprint (w-32).

layout_options.name: Option 4: The 2x2 Feature Card
layout_options.description: Uses a CSS Grid to create a 2x2 bento layout that is perfectly square-ish or rectangular, good for reading.
layout_options.structure: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-px bg-slate-200 border rounded-xl overflow-hidden
sections: Top-Left (bg-white p-5): Overall Rating and Sub-scores.
Top-Right (bg-white p-5): Tournament Stats prominently displayed.
Bottom-Full (col-span-1 md:col-span-2 bg-white p-5): The CI Visualization taking up the full width for maximum precision reading.

ci_visualization_math.concept: A horizontal bar representing the 700-2200 range. A highlighted band represents the ±77 interval. A dot represents the exact score 1677.

instructions_to_main_agent: Read this JSON carefully to understand the 4 layout options requested.
Create a new generic dashboard layout or showcase page in the frontend to display all 4 options stacked vertically with generous spacing (gap-12) so the user can review them.
Do NOT drop any data points. Every option MUST include: Overall 9/10, the 4 sub-scores with their specific colors, the 45W/4L/49M/92% tournament stats, and the CI 1677 ±77 range bar.
Implement the CI visualization accurately using absolute positioning as detailed in the 'ci_visualization_math' section.
Apply `data-testid` to all major containers and metrics (e.g., `data-testid='score-card-option-1'`, `data-testid='overall-rating'`, `data-testid='win-rate'`).
Use Lucide-react icons sparingly but effectively (e.g., Target, Trophy, Activity, TrendingUp) to accompany section labels.
Ensure complete responsiveness. Mobile views should stack cleanly (`flex-col` or `grid-cols-1`) with proper borders/dividers adjusting (e.g., switching from `divide-x` to `divide-y`).
DO NOT USE ANY PLACEHOLDER IMAGES OR TEXT. Use exactly the data provided.
